div.span: ESC8: NTLM Relay to HTTP Enrollment

Steal CA private key:
Mimikatz crypto::certificates
Export CA certificate + key

Forge certificates indefinitely
Valid until CA cert expires

Survives password changes
Account disablement
Standard remediation

CA has:
• Web enrollment enabled
• HTTP (not HTTPS)
• No EPA/SMB signing

Attacker has:
• ManageCA rights on CA
• ManageCertificates rights
• Can approve pending requests

Enable EDITF flag OR
approve pending cert request

CA has flag:
• EDITF_ATTRIBUTESUBJECTALTNAME2
• Allows SAN in CSR
• on ANY template

Request any template cert
with SAN in CSR attributes

CA honors SAN from CSR
ignoring template config

Attacker has:
• WriteProperty on template
• WriteDacl/WriteOwner
• Can modify template

Modify template properties:
Enable SAN specification
Add enrollment rights

Template allows:
• Certificate Request Agent EKU
• Enroll on behalf of others

Use to request cert
on behalf of admin

Template allows:
• Any Purpose EKU or no EKU
• Low-priv enrollment
• Can be used for anything

Template allows:
• Client Authentication EKU
• CT_FLAG_ENROLLEE_SUPPLIES_SUBJECT
• Low-priv enrollment rights
• No manager approval

Request certificate with
arbitrary SAN UPN

Certificate issued with
Administrator UPN in SAN

Export certificate with private key
.pfx file created

Try other attacks:
Kerberoasting, AS-REP roasting

Use TGT directly:
Pass-the-Ticket attacks
Access resources as admin

DCSync attack:
Extract all domain hashes

DETECTION: Event 4886-4887 Certificate request
Event 4890 CA settings changed - Unusual enrollments
certutil.exe or Certify.exe execution - Non-standard hosts/times

MITIGATION: Disable vulnerable templates - Remove enrollment permissions
Require manager approval - Monitor certificate issuance
Remove EDITF flag from CA - Enable EPA on web enrollment
